2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by GR Stocks on UnsplashIn the fast-moving world of technology, ignoring your competitors isn’t humility or focus, it’s a quiet surrender.
I’ve always seen competition as fuel rather than distraction. We don’t build in a vacuum. Markets reward the sharpest solutions, the most efficient systems, and the products that truly solve user pain.
Pretending rivals don’t exist is like optimizing code without profiling it , you’re flying blind, convinced your elegant architecture is flawless while someone else ships faster, leaner, and more reliable.
The Zero-Sum Reality Engineers Must Embrace
Engineering culture often romanticizes pure creation: “Just focus on the craft.” That mindset has its place in research, but in product development, startups, or enterprise software, it’s dangerously incomplete.
Every contract your competitor wins is engineering hours not spent on your roadmap.
Every user they retain is feedback and iteration velocity you’re missing. Every talent they attract is a colleague who could have strengthened your team.
This isn’t cynicism, it’s physics. Resources, attention, and capital are finite.
Ignoring competitors leads to blind spots: you might over-engineer features they solved elegantly with better…
